title: A method of containership allocation-based stowage planning
abstract: The invention relates to a method for containership allocation-based stowage planning, the method comprising one or more allocation slot planning phases where an intermediate slot plan is obtained, where for one or more of the phases a neighbourhood search is performed, and where for a neighbourhood search a score is generated indicating an improvement of the intermediate allocation slot plan.The invention further relates to a computer program for implementing the method on a computer.
claims: Claims P A T E N T C L A I M S 1. A computer implemented method for containership allocation-based stowage planning, the method comprising one or more allocation slot planning phases where an intermediate allocation slot plan is obtained, where for one or more of the phases a Neighbourhood search is performed, and where for a Neighbourhood search a score is generated indicating an improvement of the intermediate allocation slot plan, where in one or more phases an allocation slot plan is generated, where the Neighbourhood search is perfomed, where a score is generated for the Neighbourhood search and where an optimized allocation slot plan is generated in case the score indicates an improvement, and where an optimized container allocation slot plan is used for container placement operations. 2. A method according to claim 1 , where the input to the Neighbourhood search comprises vessel data, allocations and a loadlist. 3. A method according to claim 1 , or 2, comprising providing an allocation slot plan and evaluating feasibility of the provided plan. 4. A method according to any of the preceding claims, where in case the allocation slot plan is infeasible, containers are removed from the load list until the plan is considered feasible. 5. A method according to claim 1 -4, where the Neighbourhood search uses metaheuristic, e.g. hill climbing, simulated annealing, tabu search, or genetic algorithms (parallel/non-parallel) possibly extended with any kind of pre/post/interleaved phases. 6. A method according to claim 1 -5, where the Neighbourhood search in the primary hill climbing setup (parallel/non-parallel) possibly extended with any kind of pre/post/interleaved phases. 7. A method according to any of the preceding claims, where an allocation slot planning phase may include stowage rules such as physical constraints or seaworthiness constraints. 8. A method according to any of the preceding claims, where the improved allocation slot plan is transferred to an operational unit carrying out the stowage of the containers according to the improved plan. 9. A computer program adapted for performing the method according to any of the claims 1 -8 on a computer, the computer program being configured to perform one or more allocation slot planning phases where an intermediate allocation slot plan is obtained, where the computer program for one or more of the phases is configured to perform a neighbourhood search, and where for a Neighbourhood search the computer program is configured to generate a score indicating an improvement of the intermediate allocation slot plan, where the computer program is configured to generate, in one or more phases, an allocation slot plan, where the neighbourhood search is performed, where a score is generated by the computer program for the Neighbourhood search and where an optimized allocation slot plan is generated by the computer program in case the score indicates an improvement. 10. A computer program according to claim 9, where the input to the Neighbourhood search comprises vessel data, allocations and a loadlist. 11.A computer program according to claim 9 or 10, comprising providing an allocation slot plan and evaluating feasibility of the provided plan. 12. A computer program according to any of the preceding claims, where in case the allocation slot plan is infeasible, containers are removed from the load list until the plan is considered feasible. 13. A computer program according to claim 9-12, where the Neighbourhood search uses metaheuristic, e.g. hill climbing, simulated annealing, tabu search, or genetic algorithms (parallel/non-parallel) possibly extended with any kind of pre/post/interleaved phases. 14. A computer program according to claim 9-13, where the Neighbourhood search in the primary hill climbing setup (parallel/non- parallel) possibly extended with any kind of pre/post/interleaved phases. 15. A computer program according to any of the preceding claims, where an allocation slot planning phase may include stowage rules such as physical constraints or seaworthiness constraints. 16. A computer program according to any of the preceding claims, where the improved allocation slot plan is transferred to an operational unit carrying out the stowage of the containers according to the improved plan.
